sanskrit: अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते । तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम् ॥
hindiTranslation: जो अनन्य भक्त मेरा चिन्तन करते हुए मेरी उपासना करते हैं, मेरेमें निरन्तर लगे हुए उन भक्तोंका योगक्षेम अप्राप्तकी प्राप्ति और प्राप्तकी रक्षा मैं वहन करता हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: Those who think of me alone and worship me with unwavering focus—I carry their welfare and security.
essence: Unwavering devotion is met by unwavering support.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: People who focus on Krishna alone and worship him steadily do not have to carry every burden by themselves. He takes responsibility for their well-being and security.
simpleInsight: Devotion can feel like being held instead of having to hold everything alone.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. After exposing the limits of ritual and mixed motives, Krishna gives a promise to the devoted: those who turn to him without divided attention are personally sustained by him.
modernRelevance: You are staring at a hospital bill, a breakup text, and a job notice all at once. This verse answers the panic that says, 'I have to hold everything myself.'
translationLiteral: अनन्याः (with no other focus) / चिन्तयन्तः (thinking) / माम् (me) / ये (those who) / जनाः (people) / पर्युपासते (worship all around) / तेषाम् (for them) / नित्य-अभियुक्तानाम् (for those always joined) / योग-क्षेमम् (welfare and security) / वहामि (I carry) / अहम् (I)
